cluster: C6 — hygiene: subtractions + verification depth (dead exports, /proc parser consolidation, contract-test gaps, CI mirror alignment, pre-0.6 doc archival)

play_verdicts.play: Delete link-verified dead exports + write-never ports/volumes recipe plumbing
play_verdicts.reason: Re-verified every symbol myself this session: fresh grep over src/include/tests/scripts/.github shows definition+declaration hits only, and my own nm -o over BOTH personalities (obj/ and a freshly built obj-test/ — make test links the shipped hold from TEST_OBJS, so the test tree is the real link gate) shows every listed symbol T-only with zero U references: hold_gen_id_for_store, hold_chown_root_if_root, hold_resolve_self_executable_path, hold_open_unique_temp, hold_fsync_dir_path, hold_format_relative_age, hold_json_get_args_alloc, hold_valid_target_atom, hold_cli_command_is_public. hold_sha256_file_hex is declared at core.h:68 with no definition anywhere — a live link trap. The HOLD_TESTING ifdef bodies (invocation.c:30, layout.c:111, list.c:436, boot.c:9) reference none of them; viewer_filter_test.c's symbol list (extracted this session) uses none. hold_cli_command_is_public (cli.c:186-188) is a byte-identical twin of the live hold_cli_command_is_parser_owned (cli.c:182-184, sole caller cli_main.c:559). The ports/volumes write-never claim is FACT: -p/-v are rejected at cli_main.c:282-292, and a complete field trace shows the only writes are NULL-init (record.c:19-20,26-27), free, and pass-through — the count>0 serialization guards at record.c:282-291 are provably never true, so deletion is behavior-identical for every record the binary can produce. No test references any symbol or the recipe 'ports'/'volumes' keys (grep verified).
amendments: Add the two link-dead logging wrappers the play omits but its own backing finding lists: hold_write_json_log_entry_fd and hold_write_json_log_bytes_fd (logging.c:381-387, core.h:119-120) — nm confirms T-only in both trees. Do NOT touch hold_decode_json_log_line in this commit; that is a behavioral change belonging to a different finding.

The pass-2 sketch misses the recipe free/init sites: also delete record.c:12-13, 19-20, 26-27 (hold_free_argv_alloc/NULL-init of recipe.ports/volumes) or the build breaks on the removed struct fields.

Decision gate before deleting hold_open_unique_temp (fs.c:60-85) and hold_fsync_dir_path (fs.c:237-248): record.c:156-163/397-401 hand-roll the identical O_EXCL temp pattern, and the ledger's crash-leftover-temp/durability remedy is their natural consumer. Ask the owner: if that remedy will adopt them, keep these two and delete the rest; otherwise delete all (git preserves). Do not let this stall the commit — default is delete.

Delete only core.h:68 for the sha256 decl; do NOT cascade into the PROFILE_HASH_* macro rename or hold_valid_profile_hash fold — that is the separate types.h vocabulary finding. Keep this commit purely subtractive.

While deleting ports/volumes lines in record.c:282-291/754-761 and call.c:145-148, do not disturb the immediately adjacent cap_add/cap_drop lines (record.c:292-299, 762-770; call.c:149-152) — they are pinned by test_hold.sh:2521-2574 and owned by the unexecuted --cap cut decision.

State the record-format narrowing in the commit message/CHANGELOG: records containing 'ports'/'volumes' keys (hand-written or from pre-cut binaries) stop being replayed on redial — this is the anti-legacy-shim intent, made explicit.

Verification gate: re-run the nm cross-ref after rebase; then make (zero warnings, static), make hold-dynamic, make test (135/135 — also proves the TEST_OBJS link), make test-040 (release-personality link), make lint. Confirm test_hold.sh:3589/3596 and test_040:73/78 (-p/-v rejection pins) still pass untouched.

play_verdicts.play: Consolidate the three /proc/<pid>/stat pgid/sid/state parsers onto platform/process.c
play_verdicts.reason: Finding verified by reading all three: process.c:42-108 validates via parse_pid_token (end/errno/positivity, process.c:29-38); observe.c:45-100 and shell.c:132-181 are the same open/EINTR/strrchr(')')/strtok_r skeleton with bare strtol(tok, NULL, 10) at the pgid/sid fields. Call sites verified: observe.c:232 (group scan) and shell.c:~195 (find_process_in_pgid), both inside #if defined(__linux__) blocks, both 'continue' on parser failure. Behavior delta at the margins is benign: the strict parser rejects pgid<=0 tokens (kernel threads) where the lax copies fabricate 0 — but a 0 pgid can never match a validated target pgid>1, so scan outcomes are identical; no test can pin lax behavior (tests cannot forge /proc stat lines). Layer direction is clean (runtime→platform allowed per lint_layers.sh; shell.c:6 already includes platform.h; observe.c does NOT — the play correctly adds it). ~100 lines net negative on the safety-adjacent path, one validation policy.
amendments: Guard shape: keep process.c's existing #if !defined(__APPLE__) for the real body and add an #else ENOSYS stub for the exported hold_read_proc_group_ids. Do NOT leave the platform.h declaration definition-less on macOS — that recreates the exact hold_sha256_file_hex link-trap this cluster deletes. The stub preserves current macOS behavior (observe.c:423-427 returns ENOSYS today).
In observe.c's #else /* !__linux__ */ block, delete ONLY the hold_proc_read_ids stub; the sibling stubs hold_proc_read_cpu_rss and hold_proc_fd_target must stay.

REJECT the 'optional same-session extension' (folding hold_read_proc_stat_tokens' Linux branch, process.c:269-318, onto a shared token walk): that function feeds recycled-PID revalidation on the signal path (signal.c:115, state.c:35, start.c:1389, shell.c:558 — the SPEC safety invariant). Mixing a mechanical dedup with a safety-path rewrite in one commit is the wrong risk fusion. Named deferral D1.
Post-change nm check: hold_read_proc_group_ids defined exactly once per tree; hold_proc_read_ids and shell_read_proc_ids absent; observe.h:22 deleted.

play_verdicts.play: Pin the three unasserted contract shapes: --env-file, hold on/off, PORTS IPv6+comma cell
play_verdicts.reason: All three gaps verified: grep for env-file across tests/ returns zero hits while docker-parity-contract.md:22 lists it ('Tests assert the shapes written here', line 9); all guarded-shell tests spell the alias ('"$HOLD_BIN" shell' at test_hold.sh:3706, plus 3728/3775) so the canonical on/off dispatch (cli_main.c:717 fold, on at ~761, off at ~766) has zero coverage under its documented names; the fixture at 2749 carries '127.0.0.1:8080/tcp, [::]:9090/tcp' but the 2755 grep asserts only the IPv4 half, and list.c:311 copies observed_ports verbatim into the PORTS cell so the full-cell grep is stable. Error shapes verified in code: 'cannot read env file' (cli_main.c:301) and 'invalid --env-file entry at %s:%lu' (cli_main.c:319), both rc 5, physical-line counting so a bad entry on line 3 reports :3; banner at shell.c:704-706 prints to stderr unconditionally before spawn (works piped); off outside a session returns rc 1 with 'hold: not inside a hold on session' (shell.c:755-758). The play's self-defense against entrenching the self-setenv bug (assert child-visible env via logs, not hold's own env) is sound and required. Doc license checks out: identity doc lines 38-41 (on/off), parity contract line 22 (--env-file) and 62-63 (PORTS).
amendments: When switching the invocation at 3706 to '"$HOLD_BIN" on', keep the 'help shell' assertions at 3699-3703 and the alias spellings at 3728/3775 unchanged — the alias's fate is an owner redline (no current doc sanctions shell/console), not this commit's call. Named deferral D4.
The off test must be 'env -u HOLD_ON_PID "$HOLD_BIN" off' for determinism (a developer running make test inside a hold-on session would otherwise flip the result).

In the bad-entry test, assert only the outer 'invalid --env-file entry at .*:3' message — stderr also carries the inner 'expected KEY=VALUE after --env/-e' line from append_env_assignment; pinning both couples the test to an internal message layering.
Do not assert log length or last-N anywhere in these tests: hold logs -p truncates to the first 50 records (filter.c:6 critical finding, another cluster's fix); a single echo line is safe.

PORTS grep: extend the existing -Eq full-row regex at 2755 with '127\.0\.0\.1:8080/tcp, \[::\]:9090/tcp' (ERE bracket escaping inside the double-quoted pattern) rather than adding a second grep — one pin, one line.

play_verdicts.play: Fuse the CI gate definitions (add test-040 + version-sync to GitHub) and harden the remaining one-shot sleeps
play_verdicts.reason: Drift verified: scripts/ci.sh runs check_version_sync.sh (step 'version sync') and make test-040 (suite step), while ci.yml's build-and-test runs only make test (line 27) and release.yml's test job only make test (line 52) — grep of .github/ finds no test-040 or check_version_sync anywhere. test_040 is genuinely the only exercise of the non-HOLD_TESTING personality (Makefile:66-67, HOLD_BIN=./hold-dynamic) plus the -p/-v rejections and ps-header smoke, and release.yml gates tag publishing on jobs that never run it. The fixed sleeps are real: test_040:59/62 (0.3s before redial and before requiring exactly 2 log lines), test_macos_ci.sh:36 (0.2s, release-gating via build-macos), test_hold.sh:599/603 (one-shot pid_dead_enough vs the polling pgid_terminated at 541-553), 1877, 1888. The 'add to CI' answer beats 'fix the claim': the local mirror already runs the commands, so the cheap direction is making GitHub match. The play's own rejection of a shellcheck gate (46W/36E baseline would cry wolf) is correct discipline.
amendments: ci.yml's checkout is shallow (plain checkout@v5, line 20, no fetch-depth) — resolve_version.sh degrades to the dev/no-tag path there. It still self-agrees (both sides use the same resolver) but verifies less than locally. Either add fetch-depth: 0 to the build-and-test job or document the reduced check in CI-PARITY.md; do not add the step silently weaker. release.yml already has fetch-depth: 0.

Sketch error: the full 64-hex id is NOT 'already captured at line 57' of test_040 — line 57 pipes extract_full64 straight into cut -c1-12. Capture full64 into a variable first, derive the 12-char display from it, then poll $HOME/.local/state/hold/<full64>.json for '"state": "exited"' (record path layout confirmed by the script's own --rm check).

test_group_kill_children: for the post-stop check, use the existing pgid_terminated "$pgid" helper (children share the pgid; it already polls 40x0.05s) instead of hand-rolling per-child loops; wrap the pre-stop children ps scan in a retry loop.

Line 1877 (leader-zombie): a grep-with-retry on list output does NOT fix the real race — the 'Up' assertion passes even before the leader exits, so the test can silently stop exercising the zombie-leader path. Poll ps -o stat= on the leader pid for 'Z' first, then assert list shows Up.
Line 1888 (finished-tail): use the existing record_ended_soon "$id" helper before invoking hold tail — it both removes the race and avoids tail engaging follow-mode on a still-live call.

Carve the commit: (4a) CI alignment — ci.yml, release.yml, test_040 polls, test_macos_ci.sh retry, plus the two-line ci.sh relabel of the stale 'profile-hash vector' step wording (lines 4-5 and the suite step label — same claim-truth rationale, artifact no longer exists); (4b) test_hold.sh flake hardening (599-606, 1877, 1888) as a separate commit — those sites already run in GitHub CI today via make test, so they are pre-existing flakes, not ones 4a imports, and a YAML revert must not drag test-semantics changes with it.
Stability gate before merging 4a: run tests/test_040_dockerish.sh standalone 5x and bash scripts/ci.sh once; bash -n every edited script.

play_verdicts.play: Archive pre-0.6 docs (launcher.md/store.md/console.md) and remove the dead profile-era example script (implied by cluster focus; findings launcher.md:9 and examples/uv-webserver-profile.sh:143)
play_verdicts.reason: Verified: launcher.md:9 claims 'a bare 12-hex run ID on stdout' and 16-18 documents 'hold start <profile>' — both dead against the pinned 64-hex contract and the verb table; docs/index.md:28-32 still lists launcher/store/identity/console as current 'Technical references'; SPEC.md:7-8 cites launcher/store/identity/console as mechanism references, so archiving without editing SPEC.md and index.md would put dead links into the normative chain — worse than today. examples/uv-webserver-profile.sh:143/150 runs 'profile save' and profile start against a binary whose identity doc says 'There is no profile system.' No test pins any of this content.
amendments: git mv docs/launcher.md docs/store.md docs/console.md to docs/archive/ and, in the SAME commit, rewrite index.md:28-32 and SPEC.md:7-8 so the reference chain stays link-clean (SPEC keeps identity.md, which is not named for archive by this cluster).
identity.md's stale 'run' vocabulary is noted but left to the docs-drift cluster — do not expand scope here. Named deferral D6.

Example script: DELETE examples/uv-webserver-profile.sh and correct examples/README.md now (honest subtraction, matches 'fix or remove'); a rewritten save/rename/redial showcase needs runtime verification and is a named deferral (D3), not a rider on a docs commit.
Docs-only + example deletion; zero code, zero test changes; verify with a link check over docs/ (grep for the moved filenames outside docs/archive/) and make test untouched.

pinned_conflicts.what: Pins the guarded-shell flow via the legacy 'shell' spelling and 'help shell' output. Play 3 deliberately re-pins the invocation at 3706 to the canonical 'on' verb and adds the banner assert; the help-shell asserts and the alias spellings at 3728/3775 are intentionally left as alias coverage until the owner redlines the alias's fate.

Existing PORTS pin greps only the IPv4 half of the fixture cell planted at 2749. Play 3 tightens this pin to the full '127.0.0.1:8080/tcp, [::]:9090/tcp' cell — an extension, not a break; list.c:311 renders the projection verbatim so the tightened shape is stable.

Pin the -p/-v honest-rejection stderr shapes. Play 1 pass 2 deletes the ports/volumes plumbing BEHIND these rejections but must leave reject_publish_option/reject_volume_option (cli_main.c:282-292) and their parse branches intact, or four pinned tests break.

Pin cap_add/cap_drop record round-trip. Play 1's record.c/call.c deletions are line-adjacent to the cap serialization/reload (record.c:292-299, 762-770; call.c:149-152); a careless range delete breaks these pinned tests. The --cap cut itself is an owner decision outside this cluster.

Standalone link of filter.c+logging.c+json.c+util.c+paths.c. Verified this session that its symbol set uses none of play 1's deleted exports (it does use hold_parse_json_string and hold_resolve_binary_path — neither is on the list); this link must stay green after the deletions.

Existing tests whose timing scaffolding (fixed sleeps / one-shot checks) play 4 rewrites to polls. Assertions are preserved; only readiness detection changes. The 1877 site must be converted to a leader-state poll, not a grep retry, or the test silently stops exercising the zombie-leader path.
Normative SPEC and the docs index both cite launcher.md/store.md/console.md as current references. The archive move must edit both files in the same commit or the normative chain acquires dead links.

pinned_conflicts.where: tests/test_hold.sh group-kill/stats/ports/shell tests (e.g. 'stop kills full process group (children)', the stats and ports families, guarded-shell adoption tests)
pinned_conflicts.what: These are the behavioral net under play 2's parser consolidation — the retargeted call sites (observe.c:232, shell.c:~195) are exercised by them. No updates needed; they must run green unmodified as the consolidation's acceptance gate.

implementation_risks: 1. Cross-cluster collision on shared surfaces: play 1 pass 2 edits record.c (282-291, 754-761), call.c (145-152 region), start.c, and types.h/runtime.h lines directly adjacent to the cap_add/cap_drop plumbing that the identity-cut decision will also touch, and record.c is also the store-durability cluster's target. Land play 1 strictly before or after those clusters, never concurrently — adjacent-line merges here produce silent double-deletions.

2. Deleting hold_open_unique_temp/hold_fsync_dir_path may be immediate churn: they are the exact primitives the record.c crash-leftover-temp/durability remedy wants (record.c hand-rolls the same O_EXCL pattern at 156-163/397-401). Resolve the keep-or-delete decision with the owner before the commit; wrong default costs a delete-then-reintroduce cycle.

3. macOS link/build breakage in play 2: the exported hold_read_proc_group_ids MUST carry an #else ENOSYS stub under __APPLE__ (process.c's real body is !__APPLE__-guarded). There is no local macOS builder; the CI macOS lane is the only gate, and it runs a reduced smoke — a declared-undefined symbol would reproduce the hold_sha256_file_hex trap this very cluster deletes.

4. Struct-field removal blast radius: dropping ports/portc/volumes/volumec from hold_run_recipe/run_options recompiles every TU touching the recipe; a missed free site becomes a leak visible only under ASan. Run make test under the ASan flags (ci.sh's SAN_FLAGS) locally before pushing, since ci.yml's sanitizer job is post-merge.

5. ci.yml shallow checkout silently weakens the new version-sync gate: without fetch-depth: 0 the resolver takes the no-tag path, so the CI check verifies less than the local one while appearing identical. Add fetch-depth: 0 or document the delta; otherwise the 'self-verifying mirror' claim is false on day one.

6. test_040 poll conversion depends on capturing the full 64-hex id BEFORE the cut -c1-12 (the play's sketch wrongly says it is already captured); implementing the sketch as written leaves no record path to poll and the fix degenerates back to a sleep.

7. Play 3's env-file tests can entrench the known self-setenv bug (cli_main.c:266) if any assertion observes hold's own environment; assert only child-visible env via logs -p and the two stderr error shapes — both survive the eventual fix.

8. test-040 in GitHub CI is a new flake surface on shared runners (named-smoke redial, port smoke needing python3); merging 4a without the poll conversions, or without the 5x local stability run, converts a local-only flake into a release-gating one.
revised_change_set:
Five commits on tempo-refinement, in this order (each independently green: zero-warning make, make test, make lint).

COMMIT 1 — runtime: one /proc stat reader, one validation policy (play 2, S). Files: src/platform/process.c — un-static read_process_ids_state, rename to hold_read_proc_group_ids, keep the real body under the existing #if !defined(__APPLE__) and add an #else stub returning -1/ENOSYS (mirrors observe.c's current macOS behavior); update the two internal call sites (process.c:172, 239) and delete the forward decl (process.c:12-14). include/hold/platform.h — declare it next to hold_read_proc_stat_tokens (:19). src/runtime/observe.c — delete hold_proc_read_ids (45-100) and ONLY its stub inside the #else !__linux__ block (423-427, leaving hold_proc_read_cpu_rss/hold_proc_fd_target stubs); retarget :232; add #include "hold/platform.h" (verified absent). include/hold/observe.h — delete :22. src/runtime/shell.c — delete shell_read_proc_ids (132-181); retarget find_process_in_pgid's call (~195); platform.h already included (:6). Verify: make (zero warnings), make test (135/135; group-kill/stats/ports/shell families cover both retargeted sites), bash scripts/lint_layers.sh, nm -o over obj/ and obj-test/ shows exactly one definition and no stray references. EXPLICITLY DEFERRED (D1): folding hold_read_proc_stat_tokens' Linux branch (process.c:269-318) onto a shared token walk — it feeds recycled-PID revalidation (SPEC safety invariant).

COMMIT 2 — subtract dead exports and write-never recipe plumbing (play 1, M). Precondition: owner decision on keeping hold_open_unique_temp/hold_fsync_dir_path for the store-durability remedy (D2); default delete. Pass 1 (pure dead code): src/store/layout.c:10-11,13-21,161-263; include/hold/store.h:8,14-18; include/hold/core.h:68 (hold_sha256_file_hex — declared, defined nowhere), :28, :56, :64, :70, :119-120, :123; src/platform/paths.c:219-251 + include/hold/platform.h:27; src/core/fs.c:60-85, 237-248; src/core/util.c:178-196; src/core/json.c:428-430; src/core/validate.c:123-125; src/core/logging.c:381-387; src/cli.c:186-188 + include/hold/cli.h:10 (keep the byte-identical hold_cli_command_is_parser_owned — sole live caller cli_main.c:559). Pass 2 (ports/volumes recipe, write-never by construction): include/hold/types.h:16-19; include/hold/runtime.h:24-27; src/cli_main.c:54-57, 70-71, 93-96 (KEEP the -p/-v rejections at 282-292 — contract-pinned); src/runtime/start.c:797-800, 814-815 (ports/volumes clauses only), 1357-1364, 1513-1524 (ports/volumes frees only); src/store/record.c:12-13, 19-20, 26-27, 282-291, 754-761 (do NOT touch the adjacent cap_add/cap_drop lines); src/runtime/call.c:145-148; src/core/json.c:436-443 + include/hold/core.h:58-59. Verify: re-run the per-symbol nm cross-ref over both object trees post-rebase, make + make hold-dynamic (zero warnings), make test (135/135), make test-040, make lint, ASan-flagged make test locally; record the wc -l delta (~300 lines with the two logging wrappers) and the record-format narrowing (ports/volumes keys no longer read) in the commit message/CHANGELOG.

COMMIT 3 — pin the three contract shapes (play 3, S; tests/test_hold.sh only). Add test_env_file_sets_child_env and test_env_file_rejects_bad_entry (details in test plan); switch the invocation at :3706 to "$HOLD_BIN" on and assert the stderr banner; add test_off_outside_session_refuses; extend the :2755 PORTS grep to the full IPv6+comma cell; register the three new tests beside their families in the run_test block (~4085+). Verify: make test → 138/138.

COMMIT 4a — CI gate fusion (play 4 core). .github/workflows/ci.yml: in build-and-test add `bash scripts/check_version_sync.sh` (and fetch-depth: 0 on its checkout, or document the reduced check in docs/CI-PARITY.md) and `make test-040 CC=${{ matrix.cc }} CFLAGS=...` after make test. .github/workflows/release.yml: add `make test-040` after :52 (hold-dynamic already built in that job; checkout already fetch-depth: 0). tests/test_040_dockerish.sh: capture full64 into a variable before cutting to 12, replace the sleeps at :59/:62 with 30x0.1s polls on "$HOME/.local/state/hold/<full64>.json" for '"state": "exited"' (house idiom: the script's own polls at 83-89/101-104). .github/scripts/test_macos_ci.sh:36-41: 2s retry loop around the logs grep. scripts/ci.sh: relabel the stale 'profile-hash vector' wording (header + suite step). Verify: bash scripts/ci.sh, tests/test_040_dockerish.sh standalone x5, bash -n on all edited scripts; actionlint/yamllint if available.

COMMIT 4b — pre-existing flake hardening (carved from play 4). tests/test_hold.sh: test_group_kill_children — retry the children ps scan until non-empty, replace :603-606 with pgid_terminated "$pgid"; test_leader_zombie_group_still_running (:1877) — poll ps -o stat= on the leader pid for 'Z' before asserting the Up row (a grep retry would not exercise the intended path); test_tail_finished_log_prints_existing_output (:1888) — record_ended_soon "$id" instead of sleep. Verify: make test x3 locally.

COMMIT 5 — docs: archive the pre-0.6 references, drop the profile-era example. git mv docs/launcher.md docs/store.md docs/console.md → docs/archive/; update docs/index.md:28-32 and docs/SPEC.md:7-8 in the same commit (identity.md stays — its 'run' vocabulary is the docs-drift cluster's item, D6); delete examples/uv-webserver-profile.sh and rewrite examples/README.md honestly. Verify: grep the moved filenames outside docs/archive/ comes back empty; make test untouched.

NAMED DEFERRALS: D1 stat-tokens parser fold (signal-path risk); D2 open_unique_temp/fsync_dir_path retention pending the store-durability remedy; D3 replacement save/rename/redial example script; D4 shell/console alias fate + 'help shell' pin (owner redline); D5 shellcheck CI gate (46W/36E baseline would cry wolf; SC1087 hits verified false positives); D6 identity.md vocabulary rewrite.
test_plan: NEW test_env_file_sets_child_env (test_hold.sh, beside the docker-flag family): write "$TEST_ROOT/env.file" with a '# comment' line, a blank line, PROBE_A=alpha, PROBE_B=beta; id=$("$HOLD_BIN" --env-file "$TEST_ROOT/env.file" -d -- /bin/sh -c 'echo "$PROBE_A-$PROBE_B"' 2>&1 | extract_id); record_ended_soon "$id" || return 1; "$HOLD_BIN" logs -p "$id" | grep -qx 'alpha-beta'. Asserts child-visible env only — survives the eventual self-setenv fix. Register: run_test "--env-file loads KEY=VALUE into the child environment" test_env_file_sets_child_env.

NEW test_env_file_rejects_bad_entry: printf 'A=1

NOEQUALS
' > "$TEST_ROOT/bad.env" (NOEQUALS on physical line 3 — lineno counts skipped lines too, verified at cli_main.c:305-313); set +e run with the file, expect rc 5 and stderr matching 'invalid --env-file entry at .*:3' (do not pin the inner KEY=VALUE line); then a nonexistent path, expect rc 5 and stderr 'cannot read env file'. Register: run_test "--env-file rejects malformed entries with file:line (exit 5)" test_env_file_rejects_bad_entry.

NEW test_off_outside_session_refuses: set +e; env -u HOLD_ON_PID "$HOLD_BIN" off 2>"$TEST_ROOT/off.err"; rc=$?; set -e; [ "$rc" -eq 1 ] and grep -q 'not inside a hold on session' "$TEST_ROOT/off.err". Register: run_test "off outside a hold on session refuses (exit 1)" test_off_outside_session_refuses.

UPDATE test_hold_shell_runs_real_shell_without_creating_runid_on_exit (:3697): change the :3706 invocation from '"$HOLD_BIN" shell' to '"$HOLD_BIN" on'; add grep -q 'Hold is now active' "$TEST_ROOT/hold-shell.err" (banner verified at shell.c:704-706, stderr, printed before spawn so pipe-driven runs see it); keep the help-shell assertions and the alias spellings at :3728/:3775 as alias coverage.

UPDATE :2755: extend the existing -Eq row regex to include '127\.0\.0\.1:8080/tcp, \[::\]:9090/tcp' — pins the Docker comma-join and IPv6 bracket cell (contract lines 62-63) against the fixture already planted at :2749.

UPDATE test_group_kill_children (:595-607): wrap the children ps scan in a 10x0.1s retry until non-empty; after stop, replace the sleep + per-child pid_dead_enough with pgid_terminated "$pgid" (existing 40x0.05s helper; children share the pgid).

UPDATE test_leader_zombie_group_still_running (:1877): replace sleep 0.3 with a poll of ps -o stat= -p <leader pid from the record> until 'Z' (bounded, ~40x0.05s), THEN assert the list row shows Up — a plain grep retry would pass before the zombie state and stop exercising the intended path.
UPDATE test_tail_finished_log_prints_existing_output (:1888): replace sleep 0.2 with record_ended_soon "$id" before invoking hold tail (also prevents tail follow-mode on a live call).

UPDATE tests/test_040_dockerish.sh (:57-63): full=$(hold -d --name named-smoke ... | extract_full64); named=${full:0:12}; replace both sleep 0.3 with 'for _ in $(seq 1 30); do grep -q "\"state\": \"exited\"" "$HOME/.local/state/hold/$full.json" && break; sleep 0.1; done' (record path layout verified against the script's own --rm check); same poll before the 2-line log-count assertion after redial (using the redial's full id).
UPDATE .github/scripts/test_macos_ci.sh (:36-41): replace sleep 0.2 with a 2s retry loop (20x0.1s) around the 'mac-smoke-line' logs grep, matching the ps-port poll idiom in test_040:83-89.

NO new tests for commits 1 and 2 (this is the honest answer, not a gap): the deletions are behavior-identical by construction — no shell test can fabricate a malformed /proc stat line to pin strict-vs-lax parsing, and dead exports have no behavior to pin. Acceptance is the existing suite: make test 135/135 (138/138 after commit 3), make test-040, viewer-filter-test link, scripts/lint_layers.sh clean, zero-warning make on both personalities, and the per-symbol nm cross-reference over obj/ AND obj-test/ re-run at commit time.
STABILITY GATES: bash scripts/ci.sh end-to-end after commit 4a; tests/test_040_dockerish.sh standalone x5; make test x3 after commit 4b; bash -n on every edited shell script.

EXPLICITLY NOT PINNED (would freeze known bugs slated for other clusters): logs -p dump length / --tail last-N (filter.c:6 critical), hold list -h / ps -h behavior, --privileged acceptance, --cap-add/--cap-drop surface, and the shell/console aliases beyond existing coverage.
